title: begonia × margaritacea
intro: Begonia × margaritacea, also known as Begonia x corallina or Marguerite Begonia, is a hybrid plant species of the Begonia genus, known for its attractive white, pink, or red flowers and decorative foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Begonia × margaritacea include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions.
